_meta.name: Rattlesnake Universal Design & Conversion System
_meta.description: COMPLETE system: UI component recipes, page layouts, color palettes, typography, anti-slop rules, psychological conversion frameworks, animation patterns, responsive breakpoints, CTA formulas, objection handling, and post-build scan patterns. This single file contains everything a model needs to build a production-grade, modern, high-converting app. Models ASSEMBLE from these patterns. They do not design. They do not write conversion copy from scratch. They follow the blueprints.
_meta.usage: Injected into model context via _build_design_context(). The BUILD SPEC generator selects relevant subsets based on page types detected.

philosophy: Models don't design. They assemble. Every visual decision is pre-made.
If the model has to choose a color, spacing, or shadow — the system failed.
Modern means: restraint, hierarchy, density where useful, air where needed.
The best SaaS UIs (Linear, Vercel, Stripe, Raycast) share traits: dark-first, tight typography, minimal borders, zero decorative shadows, surgical use of color.

A $500K/yr engineer builds: loading states for every async operation, error boundaries around every data fetch, keyboard navigation on every interactive element, responsive layouts that don't break, and transitions that feel intentional.
Every page has exactly ONE primary action. If there are two equal-weight CTAs, the design is wrong.
Data density is a feature, not a bug. Show more information in less space. Users prefer scanning to clicking.
Whitespace is structural, not decorative. Use it to separate groups, not to fill emptiness.
anti_slop_rules: NEVER use shadow-lg, shadow-xl, or shadow-2xl on cards or containers. Use shadow-sm or border ONLY. Pick one, not both.
NEVER use rounded-2xl or rounded-3xl. Maximum is rounded-xl for modals. Use rounded-lg for cards, rounded-md for buttons/inputs.
NEVER use gradient backgrounds on containers or cards. Solid colors only.
NEVER use raw Tailwind color classes (gray-100, blue-500, slate-200, etc.). ONLY use CSS custom properties from the design system.
NEVER center-align body text or paragraph content. Left-align everything except single-line hero headings.
NEVER use emoji or placeholder illustrations as section icons. Use Lucide icons or nothing.
NEVER use more than 2 font weights on a single component (regular + semibold). Add medium only for navigation.
NEVER put a shadow AND a border on the same element. Choose one.
NEVER make all cards in a grid the same visual weight. Primary content gets more space.
NEVER use padding larger than p-6 on cards. p-4 or p-5 is correct for most cards.
NEVER use text-center on cards or list items. Left-align content.
NEVER use bright saturated colors for backgrounds. Backgrounds are always near-black, near-white, or muted.
NEVER use more than ONE accent color. Pick one. Use it sparingly — badges, active states, primary buttons, links. That's it.
NEVER stack multiple hover effects (scale + shadow + color change). One subtle transition per element.
NEVER use placeholder text like 'Lorem ipsum' or 'Description goes here'. Use realistic sample data.
NEVER use Comic Sans, Papyrus, or any decorative font for body text.
NEVER render a page without a loading state. Every data fetch shows a skeleton loader or spinner.
NEVER show a raw error message from the server. Wrap errors in a user-friendly alert component.
NEVER build a form without inline validation feedback. Show errors per-field, not just a top-level banner.
NEVER build a list without an empty state. If there are zero items, show a message + CTA to create the first one.
NEVER build a delete action without a confirmation step. Use a modal or inline confirm.
NEVER hardcode widths on text containers. Use max-w-* constraints and let content flow.
NEVER ship a page that only works at desktop width. Every layout must degrade gracefully to mobile.
NEVER put clickable elements closer than 8px apart on mobile. Touch targets need min 44px height.
NEVER use onClick on a div without role='button' and tabIndex={0} and onKeyDown for Enter/Space.
NEVER show a success action without feedback. Toast, inline message, or redirect — the user must know it worked.
NEVER build a multi-step form without progress indication. Show which step the user is on and how many remain.
NEVER auto-close a toast with important information in less than 5 seconds. Error toasts should require manual dismiss.
NEVER render timestamps without relative formatting. Show '2 hours ago' not '2026-04-03T14:22:00Z'.

palettes._instructions: Each build gets ONE palette. The scaffold picks it based on template or randomly. The model ONLY uses these CSS variable names — never raw hex values in component code.

midnight.description: Dark-first. Linear/Vercel energy. Professional, dense, focused.

bone.description: Light-first. Stripe/Notion energy. Clean, warm, editorial.

carbon.description: Dark-first. GitHub/Raycast energy. Neutral, technical, no-nonsense.

sand.description: Light-first. Minimal, warm, premium. High-end agency.

obsidian.description: Ultra-dark. Moody, cinematic, high-contrast. Luxury SaaS.

typography._instructions: Each build gets ONE font pairing. Injected as Google Fonts imports in layout.tsx. Model never chooses fonts.

component_recipes._instructions: Each recipe is a complete, copy-paste pattern. The model references these by name and uses the class strings EXACTLY. Do not improvise. Every component includes hover states, transitions, loading states, and error states where applicable.

stat_card.description: Single metric display. Used in dashboard stat rows. Max 4 per row.
stat_card.label: text-xs font-medium uppercase tracking-wider text-[var(--text-tertiary)] mb-1

change.positive: text-xs font-medium text-[var(--success)] mt-1 flex items-center gap-1
change.negative: text-xs font-medium text-[var(--error)] mt-1 flex items-center gap-1

stat_card.notes: No shadow. No icon in the card. Label + number + optional change + optional sparkline. Use tabular-nums for numbers so they don't shift on update.

data_table.description: Primary data display for lists of entities. Handles sorting, selection, pagination.
data_table.th: text-left text-xs font-medium uppercase tracking-wider text-[var(--text-tertiary)] py-3 px-4 select-none
data_table.th_sortable: text-left text-xs font-medium uppercase tracking-wider text-[var(--text-tertiary)] py-3 px-4 cursor-pointer hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] transition-colors duration-100 select-none group
data_table.th_numeric: text-right text-xs font-medium uppercase tracking-wider text-[var(--text-tertiary)] py-3 px-4
data_table.checkbox: w-4 h-4 rounded border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] accent-[var(--accent)]
data_table.pagination: flex items-center justify-between px-4 py-3 border-t border-[var(--border)]
data_table.pagination_btn: px-3 py-1.5 rounded-md text-sm text-[var(--text-secondary)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] transition-colors duration-100
data_table.pagination_btn_active: px-3 py-1.5 rounded-md text-sm bg-[var(--accent-muted)] text-[var(--accent)] font-medium
data_table.notes: No outer border on the table. No zebra striping. Hover rows. Right-align numbers. Sortable headers show icon on hover. Always include pagination for >10 rows.

sidebar.description: Fixed left navigation. Always visible on desktop, drawer on mobile.
sidebar.container: fixed left-0 top-0 h-screen w-64 bg-[var(--bg-secondary)] border-r border-[var(--border)] flex flex-col z-40
sidebar.logo_area: h-14 flex items-center px-4 border-b border-[var(--border)]
sidebar.nav_section_label: text-xs font-medium uppercase tracking-wider text-[var(--text-muted)] px-4 pt-6 pb-2
sidebar.nav_item: flex items-center gap-3 px-4 py-2 mx-2 rounded-md text-sm text-[var(--text-secondary)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] transition-colors duration-100 cursor-pointer
sidebar.nav_item_active: flex items-center gap-3 px-4 py-2 mx-2 rounded-md text-sm text-[var(--text-primary)] bg-[var(--accent-muted)] font-medium
sidebar.nav_item_with_count: flex items-center gap-3 px-4 py-2 mx-2 rounded-md text-sm text-[var(--text-secondary)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] transition-colors duration-100 cursor-pointer [span.ml-auto.text-xs.tabular-nums.text-[var(--text-muted)]]
sidebar.user_area: mt-auto p-4 border-t border-[var(--border)] flex items-center gap-3
sidebar.user_avatar: w-8 h-8 rounded-full bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] flex items-center justify-center text-xs font-medium text-[var(--text-secondary)]
sidebar.mobile_close: absolute top-4 right-4 w-8 h-8 flex items-center justify-center rounded-md hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] transition-colors duration-100
sidebar.collapse_btn: hidden lg:flex items-center justify-center w-6 h-6 rounded-md absolute -right-3 top-7 bg-[var(--bg-secondary)] border border-[var(--border)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] cursor-pointer z-50
sidebar.notes: No shadow. Border-right only. Active item uses accent-muted bg. Mobile: overlay + slide-in drawer. Include collapse button for desktop. Notification counts right-aligned in nav items.

header.description: Top bar inside main content area. Contains page title, breadcrumbs, actions.
header.container: h-14 flex items-center justify-between px-6 border-b border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--bg-primary)] sticky top-0 z-20
header.title: text-lg font-semibold text-[var(--text-primary)]
header.mobile_menu_btn: lg:hidden w-9 h-9 flex items-center justify-center rounded-md hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] transition-colors duration-100
header.notes: Sticky top-0 z-20. Border-bottom only, no shadow. Mobile: include hamburger menu button to toggle sidebar.

button.primary: inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 px-4 py-2 rounded-md text-sm font-medium bg-[var(--accent)] text-white hover:bg-[var(--accent-hover)] transition-colors duration-150 focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-[var(--accent)] focus-visible:ring-offset-2 focus-visible:ring-offset-[var(--bg-primary)] disabled:opacity-50 disabled:cursor-not-allowed
button.secondary: inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 px-4 py-2 rounded-md text-sm font-medium border border-[var(--border)] text-[var(--text-primary)] bg-transparent hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] transition-colors duration-150 disabled:opacity-50 disabled:cursor-not-allowed
button.ghost: inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 px-4 py-2 rounded-md text-sm font-medium text-[var(--text-secondary)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] transition-colors duration-150 disabled:opacity-50 disabled:cursor-not-allowed
button.danger: inline-flex items-center justify-center gap-2 px-4 py-2 rounded-md text-sm font-medium bg-[var(--error)] text-white hover:opacity-90 transition-opacity duration-150 disabled:opacity-50 disabled:cursor-not-allowed
button.icon_only: inline-flex items-center justify-center w-9 h-9 rounded-md text-[var(--text-secondary)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] transition-colors duration-150
button.loading_spinner: w-4 h-4 border-2 border-current border-t-transparent rounded-full animate-spin
button.notes: No shadow. No gradient. No rounded-full except avatar buttons. Always include disabled state. Loading state replaces label text with spinner + 'Loading...' or action verb ('Saving...').

input.text: w-full px-3 py-2 rounded-md text-sm bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] border border-[var(--border)] text-[var(--text-primary)] placeholder:text-[var(--text-muted)] focus:outline-none focus:border-[var(--accent)] focus:ring-1 focus:ring-[var(--accent)] transition-colors duration-150 disabled:opacity-50 disabled:cursor-not-allowed
input.with_icon_left: w-full pl-9 pr-3 py-2 rounded-md text-sm bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] border border-[var(--border)] text-[var(--text-primary)] placeholder:text-[var(--text-muted)] focus:outline-none focus:border-[var(--accent)] focus:ring-1 focus:ring-[var(--accent)] transition-colors duration-150
input.with_addon: flex rounded-md overflow-hidden border border-[var(--border)] focus-within:border-[var(--accent)] focus-within:ring-1 focus-within:ring-[var(--accent)]
input.addon_left: px-3 py-2 bg-[var(--bg-secondary)] border-r border-[var(--border)] text-sm text-[var(--text-tertiary)] flex items-center
input.textarea: w-full px-3 py-2 rounded-md text-sm bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] border border-[var(--border)] text-[var(--text-primary)] placeholder:text-[var(--text-muted)] focus:outline-none focus:border-[var(--accent)] focus:ring-1 focus:ring-[var(--accent)] transition-colors duration-150 resize-y min-h-[100px]
input.label: block text-sm font-medium text-[var(--text-secondary)] mb-1.5
input.error_text: text-xs text-[var(--error)] mt-1 flex items-center gap-1
input.notes: Input bg is bg-tertiary for depth. Error state: red border + error message below. Success state: green border. Always show character count for textarea if max length exists. Required fields show red asterisk after label.

select.trigger: w-full px-3 py-2 rounded-md text-sm bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] border border-[var(--border)] text-[var(--text-primary)] focus:outline-none focus:border-[var(--accent)] focus:ring-1 focus:ring-[var(--accent)] transition-colors duration-150 appearance-none cursor-pointer
select.multi_select_tag: inline-flex items-center gap-1 px-2 py-0.5 rounded-md text-xs bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] border border-[var(--border)] text-[var(--text-primary)]
select.notes: Same styling as text input. Add chevron icon via positioned SVG. Multi-select shows tags in the input area.

checkbox.input: w-4 h-4 rounded border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] accent-[var(--accent)] cursor-pointer
checkbox.label: text-sm text-[var(--text-primary)] select-none group-hover:text-[var(--text-primary)]

radio.input: w-4 h-4 rounded-full border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] accent-[var(--accent)] cursor-pointer
radio.label: text-sm text-[var(--text-primary)] select-none

toggle_switch.track_off: w-9 h-5 rounded-full bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] border border-[var(--border)] transition-colors duration-200 relative
toggle_switch.track_on: w-9 h-5 rounded-full bg-[var(--accent)] transition-colors duration-200 relative
toggle_switch.thumb: absolute top-0.5 left-0.5 w-4 h-4 rounded-full bg-white transition-transform duration-200 shadow-sm
toggle_switch.label: text-sm text-[var(--text-primary)]

badge.default: inline-flex items-center px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full text-xs font-medium bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] text-[var(--text-secondary)]
badge.accent: inline-flex items-center px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full text-xs font-medium bg-[var(--accent-muted)] text-[var(--accent)]
badge.success: inline-flex items-center px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full text-xs font-medium bg-[var(--success-muted)] text-[var(--success)]
badge.warning: inline-flex items-center px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full text-xs font-medium bg-[var(--warning-muted)] text-[var(--warning)]
badge.error: inline-flex items-center px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full text-xs font-medium bg-[var(--error-muted)] text-[var(--error)]
badge.info: inline-flex items-center px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full text-xs font-medium bg-[var(--info-muted)] text-[var(--info)]
badge.removable: inline-flex items-center gap-1 px-2 py-0.5 rounded-full text-xs font-medium [button.ml-0.5.hover:opacity-70]
badge.notes: Always rounded-full. Use semantic muted bg for colored variants. Dot indicator for status badges (online/offline/busy). Removable badges include an X button.

modal.overlay: fixed inset-0 bg-black/60 backdrop-blur-sm z-50 flex items-center justify-center p-4
modal.container: w-full max-w-lg rounded-xl border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--bg-secondary)] shadow-xl p-0 animate-in fade-in zoom-in-95
modal.container_sm: w-full max-w-sm rounded-xl border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--bg-secondary)] shadow-xl p-0
modal.container_lg: w-full max-w-2xl rounded-xl border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--bg-secondary)] shadow-xl p-0
modal.container_fullscreen: w-full h-full max-w-none rounded-none border-0 bg-[var(--bg-secondary)]
modal.header: flex items-center justify-between px-6 py-4 border-b border-[var(--border)]
modal.close_btn: w-8 h-8 flex items-center justify-center rounded-md text-[var(--text-muted)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] transition-colors duration-100
modal.footer: flex items-center justify-end gap-2 px-6 py-4 border-t border-[var(--border)]
modal.footer_left_aligned: flex items-center justify-between px-6 py-4 border-t border-[var(--border)]
modal.notes: Modal is the ONE place rounded-xl is acceptable. Uses shadow-xl (only element that should). Overlay has backdrop-blur. Close on Escape key. Close on overlay click. Trap focus inside modal.

confirmation_dialog.container: w-full max-w-sm rounded-xl border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--bg-secondary)] shadow-xl p-6
confirmation_dialog.icon: w-12 h-12 mx-auto rounded-full bg-[var(--error-muted)] flex items-center justify-center text-[var(--error)] mb-4
confirmation_dialog.title: text-base font-semibold text-[var(--text-primary)] text-center
confirmation_dialog.cancel_btn: flex-1 px-4 py-2 rounded-md text-sm font-medium border border-[var(--border)] text-[var(--text-primary)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] transition-colors duration-150
confirmation_dialog.confirm_btn: flex-1 px-4 py-2 rounded-md text-sm font-medium bg-[var(--error)] text-white hover:opacity-90 transition-opacity duration-150
confirmation_dialog.notes: Full-width buttons. Cancel on left, destructive action on right. Always name the action specifically ('Delete project' not 'Confirm').

empty_state.container: flex flex-col items-center justify-center py-16 px-4
empty_state.title: text-base font-medium text-[var(--text-primary)] mb-1
empty_state.notes: Use a relevant Lucide icon. Keep copy concise. Primary CTA button below description. EVERY list/table must have this for zero-item state.

search_bar.input: w-full pl-9 pr-4 py-2 rounded-md text-sm bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] border border-[var(--border)] text-[var(--text-primary)] placeholder:text-[var(--text-muted)] focus:outline-none focus:border-[var(--accent)] focus:ring-1 focus:ring-[var(--accent)] transition-colors duration-150
search_bar.icon: absolute left-3 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 w-4 h-4 text-[var(--text-muted)]
search_bar.kbd_shortcut: absolute right-3 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 text-xs text-[var(--text-muted)] bg-[var(--bg-primary)] border border-[var(--border)] rounded px-1.5 py-0.5 font-mono
search_bar.clear_btn: absolute right-3 top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 w-4 h-4 text-[var(--text-muted)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] cursor-pointer
search_bar.results_dropdown: absolute top-full left-0 right-0 mt-1 rounded-lg border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--bg-secondary)] shadow-lg max-h-80 overflow-y-auto z-30
search_bar.result_item: flex items-center gap-3 px-4 py-2.5 text-sm text-[var(--text-secondary)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] cursor-pointer
search_bar.notes: Search icon inside input. Keyboard shortcut badge (CMD+K) or clear button on right — never both. Debounce search input by 300ms. Show loading spinner in results while fetching.

dropdown_menu.container: min-w-[180px] rounded-lg border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--bg-secondary)] shadow-lg p-1 z-50
dropdown_menu.item: flex items-center gap-2 w-full px-3 py-2 rounded-md text-sm text-[var(--text-secondary)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] transition-colors duration-100 cursor-pointer
dropdown_menu.item_with_shortcut: flex items-center justify-between w-full px-3 py-2 rounded-md text-sm text-[var(--text-secondary)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] transition-colors duration-100 cursor-pointer
dropdown_menu.item_destructive: flex items-center gap-2 w-full px-3 py-2 rounded-md text-sm text-[var(--error)] hover:bg-[var(--error-muted)] transition-colors duration-100 cursor-pointer
dropdown_menu.notes: Rounded-lg for dropdowns. p-1 outer padding with rounded-md items inside. Close on click outside. Close on Escape. Keyboard arrow navigation.

card.interactive: rounded-lg border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--surface)] p-5 hover:border-[var(--border)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] transition-colors duration-150 cursor-pointer
card.header_action: text-sm text-[var(--text-tertiary)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] transition-colors duration-100 cursor-pointer
card.footer: mt-4 pt-4 border-t border-[var(--border-subtle)] flex items-center justify-between
card.notes: No shadow. Border only. p-5 standard padding. Interactive cards change bg on hover. Selected cards use accent border.

avatar.xs: w-5 h-5 rounded-full bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] flex items-center justify-center text-[8px] font-medium text-[var(--text-secondary)] shrink-0
avatar.sm: w-6 h-6 rounded-full bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] flex items-center justify-center text-[10px] font-medium text-[var(--text-secondary)] shrink-0
avatar.md: w-8 h-8 rounded-full bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] flex items-center justify-center text-xs font-medium text-[var(--text-secondary)] shrink-0
avatar.lg: w-10 h-10 rounded-full bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] flex items-center justify-center text-sm font-medium text-[var(--text-secondary)] shrink-0
avatar.xl: w-16 h-16 rounded-full bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] flex items-center justify-center text-xl font-medium text-[var(--text-secondary)] shrink-0
avatar.group_overflow: w-8 h-8 rounded-full bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] border-2 border-[var(--bg-primary)] flex items-center justify-center text-[10px] font-medium text-[var(--text-muted)]
avatar.status_indicator: absolute bottom-0 right-0 w-2.5 h-2.5 rounded-full border-2 border-[var(--bg-primary)]
avatar.notes: Show initials by default. When image: object-cover rounded-full. Group: -space-x-2. Overflow: '+3' counter. Status dot positioned absolute bottom-right.

tabs.tab: px-4 py-2.5 text-sm font-medium text-[var(--text-tertiary)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] transition-colors duration-100 relative cursor-pointer
tabs.tab_active: px-4 py-2.5 text-sm font-medium text-[var(--text-primary)] relative cursor-pointer after:absolute after:bottom-0 after:left-0 after:right-0 after:h-0.5 after:bg-[var(--accent)]
tabs.tab_with_count: px-4 py-2.5 text-sm font-medium text-[var(--text-tertiary)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] transition-colors duration-100 relative cursor-pointer flex items-center gap-2
tabs.tab_count: text-xs tabular-nums px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded-full bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] text-[var(--text-muted)]
tabs.pill_container: flex items-center gap-1 bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] p-1 rounded-lg
tabs.pill_tab: px-3 py-1.5 text-sm font-medium text-[var(--text-tertiary)] rounded-md transition-colors duration-100 cursor-pointer
tabs.pill_tab_active: px-3 py-1.5 text-sm font-medium text-[var(--text-primary)] bg-[var(--surface)] rounded-md shadow-sm
tabs.notes: Underline tabs for primary navigation. Pill tabs for view switching (List/Board/Calendar). Active tab: accent underline. Counts show in tabular-nums.

toast.container: fixed bottom-4 right-4 z-[100] flex flex-col gap-2 pointer-events-none
toast.toast_base: flex items-start gap-3 px-4 py-3 rounded-lg border bg-[var(--bg-secondary)] shadow-lg text-sm min-w-[320px] max-w-[420px] pointer-events-auto
toast.title: font-medium text-[var(--text-primary)]
toast.close: w-5 h-5 shrink-0 text-[var(--text-muted)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] cursor-pointer mt-0.5
toast.progress_bar: absolute bottom-0 left-0 h-0.5 bg-[var(--accent)] rounded-b-lg transition-all duration-100
toast.notes: Bottom-right. Shadow-lg OK for toasts. Auto-dismiss after 5s (info) or manual dismiss (errors). Progress bar shows remaining time. Stack from bottom.

kanban_column.container: flex-shrink-0 w-72 flex flex-col bg-[var(--bg-secondary)] rounded-lg max-h-[calc(100vh-160px)]
kanban_column.header: flex items-center justify-between px-3 py-2.5 sticky top-0 bg-[var(--bg-secondary)] rounded-t-lg z-10
kanban_column.header_title: text-sm font-medium text-[var(--text-primary)] flex items-center gap-2
kanban_column.header_count: text-xs text-[var(--text-muted)] bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] rounded-full px-2 py-0.5 min-w-[20px] text-center tabular-nums
kanban_column.header_menu: w-6 h-6 flex items-center justify-center rounded-md text-[var(--text-muted)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] cursor-pointer
kanban_column.card_list: flex flex-col gap-2 px-2 pb-2 min-h-[100px] overflow-y-auto
kanban_column.task_card: p-3 rounded-md border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--surface)] hover:border-[var(--accent)] transition-colors duration-100 cursor-grab active:cursor-grabbing
kanban_column.task_card_dragging: p-3 rounded-md border border-[var(--accent)] bg-[var(--surface)] shadow-lg opacity-90 rotate-2
kanban_column.drop_zone: border-2 border-dashed border-[var(--accent)]/30 rounded-md min-h-[80px] flex items-center justify-center text-sm text-[var(--text-muted)]
kanban_column.add_button: mx-2 mb-2 px-3 py-1.5 rounded-md text-sm text-[var(--text-tertiary)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] transition-colors duration-100 cursor-pointer text-center
kanban_column.notes: Columns w-72, flex-shrink-0 for horizontal scroll. Cards use cursor-grab/cursor-grabbing. Drop zone visible during drag. Column scrolls independently if content overflows.

skeleton_loader.notes: Use in EVERY component while data loads. Match skeleton shape to expected content. Page-level skeleton: stat skeletons + table row skeletons. Never show a blank page.

command_palette.overlay: fixed inset-0 bg-black/50 backdrop-blur-sm z-50 flex items-start justify-center pt-[20vh]
command_palette.container: w-full max-w-xl rounded-xl border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--bg-secondary)] shadow-2xl overflow-hidden
command_palette.input_wrapper: flex items-center gap-3 px-4 border-b border-[var(--border)]
command_palette.input: flex-1 py-3.5 text-sm bg-transparent text-[var(--text-primary)] placeholder:text-[var(--text-muted)] focus:outline-none
command_palette.result_item: flex items-center gap-3 px-4 py-2.5 text-sm text-[var(--text-secondary)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] cursor-pointer
command_palette.result_item_active: flex items-center gap-3 px-4 py-2.5 text-sm text-[var(--text-primary)] bg-[var(--surface-hover)]
command_palette.section_label: px-4 py-1.5 text-xs font-medium uppercase tracking-wider text-[var(--text-muted)]
command_palette.footer: flex items-center justify-between px-4 py-2 border-t border-[var(--border)] text-xs text-[var(--text-muted)]
command_palette.notes: CMD+K to open. Keyboard arrow navigation. Enter to select. Escape to close. Sections group results (Pages, Actions, Settings). Include in every dashboard app.

alert_banner.info: flex items-start gap-3 px-4 py-3 rounded-lg bg-[var(--info-muted)] border border-[var(--info)]/20 text-sm text-[var(--text-primary)]
alert_banner.success: flex items-start gap-3 px-4 py-3 rounded-lg bg-[var(--success-muted)] border border-[var(--success)]/20 text-sm text-[var(--text-primary)]
alert_banner.warning: flex items-start gap-3 px-4 py-3 rounded-lg bg-[var(--warning-muted)] border border-[var(--warning)]/20 text-sm text-[var(--text-primary)]
alert_banner.error: flex items-start gap-3 px-4 py-3 rounded-lg bg-[var(--error-muted)] border border-[var(--error)]/20 text-sm text-[var(--text-primary)]
alert_banner.title: font-medium mb-0.5
alert_banner.dismiss: w-5 h-5 shrink-0 text-[var(--text-muted)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] cursor-pointer mt-0.5
alert_banner.notes: Top of page or inline. Dismissable with X button. Error alerts are not auto-dismissable.

progress_bar.fill: h-full rounded-full bg-[var(--accent)] transition-all duration-500 ease-out
progress_bar.label: text-sm font-medium text-[var(--text-primary)]
progress_bar.notes: Use for uploads, onboarding completion, quotas. Animate width transition. Show percentage or fraction (3/10).

stepper.step_complete: w-8 h-8 rounded-full bg-[var(--accent)] flex items-center justify-center text-white text-sm
stepper.step_current: w-8 h-8 rounded-full border-2 border-[var(--accent)] flex items-center justify-center text-[var(--accent)] text-sm font-medium
stepper.step_upcoming: w-8 h-8 rounded-full bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] flex items-center justify-center text-[var(--text-muted)] text-sm
stepper.notes: Use for multi-step forms, onboarding, checkout. Completed steps show checkmark. Current step has accent border. Upcoming steps are muted.

tooltip.container: absolute z-50 px-2.5 py-1.5 rounded-md bg-[var(--bg-secondary)] border border-[var(--border)] shadow-lg text-xs text-[var(--text-primary)] max-w-[200px] pointer-events-none
tooltip.arrow: absolute w-2 h-2 bg-[var(--bg-secondary)] border border-[var(--border)] rotate-45
tooltip.notes: Show on hover after 300ms delay. Position above by default, flip if near viewport edge. Max width 200px, text wraps.

breadcrumb.notes: Use / or > as separator. Last item is current page (not clickable, bolder). Truncate middle items on mobile with '...'.

file_upload.dropzone: border-2 border-dashed border-[var(--border)] rounded-lg p-8 text-center hover:border-[var(--accent)] hover:bg-[var(--accent-muted)] transition-all duration-150 cursor-pointer
file_upload.dropzone_active: border-2 border-dashed border-[var(--accent)] rounded-lg p-8 text-center bg-[var(--accent-muted)]
file_upload.title: text-sm font-medium text-[var(--text-primary)] mb-1
file_upload.file_item: flex items-center gap-3 px-3 py-2.5 rounded-md bg-[var(--surface)] border border-[var(--border)]
file_upload.file_icon: w-8 h-8 rounded-md bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] flex items-center justify-center text-xs font-medium text-[var(--text-secondary)]
file_upload.file_remove: w-6 h-6 flex items-center justify-center rounded-md text-[var(--text-muted)] hover:text-[var(--error)] hover:bg-[var(--error-muted)] transition-colors duration-100
file_upload.notes: Drag-and-drop zone with click fallback. Show file type icon based on extension. Progress bar during upload. Remove button per file.

date_picker.trigger: flex items-center gap-2 px-3 py-2 rounded-md text-sm bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] border border-[var(--border)] text-[var(--text-primary)] hover:border-[var(--accent)] transition-colors duration-150 cursor-pointer
date_picker.calendar_container: rounded-lg border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--bg-secondary)] shadow-lg p-3 w-[280px]
date_picker.calendar_nav: w-7 h-7 flex items-center justify-center rounded-md text-[var(--text-secondary)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] cursor-pointer
date_picker.calendar_day: w-9 h-9 flex items-center justify-center rounded-md text-sm text-[var(--text-primary)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] cursor-pointer transition-colors duration-100
date_picker.calendar_day_today: w-9 h-9 flex items-center justify-center rounded-md text-sm font-medium text-[var(--accent)] hover:bg-[var(--accent-muted)] cursor-pointer
date_picker.calendar_day_selected: w-9 h-9 flex items-center justify-center rounded-md text-sm font-medium bg-[var(--accent)] text-white
date_picker.calendar_day_disabled: w-9 h-9 flex items-center justify-center rounded-md text-sm text-[var(--text-muted)] cursor-not-allowed
date_picker.calendar_day_range: w-9 h-9 flex items-center justify-center text-sm text-[var(--text-primary)] bg-[var(--accent-muted)]
date_picker.notes: Calendar icon in trigger. Today highlighted with accent text. Selected date filled with accent bg. Range selection highlights in-between days with accent-muted.

timeline.line: absolute left-3 top-0 bottom-0 w-px bg-[var(--border)]
timeline.dot: absolute left-[-21px] top-1 w-6 h-6 rounded-full border-2 border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--bg-primary)] flex items-center justify-center
timeline.dot_complete: absolute left-[-21px] top-1 w-6 h-6 rounded-full bg-[var(--accent)] flex items-center justify-center text-white
timeline.title: text-sm font-medium text-[var(--text-primary)]
timeline.notes: Vertical timeline with dots on left line. Use for activity feeds, order tracking, project milestones.

notification_list.container: w-full max-w-sm rounded-lg border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--bg-secondary)] shadow-lg overflow-hidden
notification_list.header: flex items-center justify-between px-4 py-3 border-b border-[var(--border)]
notification_list.item_unread: flex gap-3 px-4 py-3 bg-[var(--accent-muted)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] transition-colors duration-100 cursor-pointer border-b border-[var(--border-subtle)]
notification_list.item_read: flex gap-3 px-4 py-3 hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] transition-colors duration-100 cursor-pointer border-b border-[var(--border-subtle)]
notification_list.unread_dot: w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-[var(--accent)] shrink-0 mt-1.5
notification_list.notes: Unread items have accent-muted bg + dot. Click marks as read. 'Mark all read' in header.

chat_message.bubble_sent: max-w-[70%] px-4 py-2.5 rounded-2xl rounded-br-md bg-[var(--accent)] text-white text-sm
chat_message.bubble_received: max-w-[70%] px-4 py-2.5 rounded-2xl rounded-bl-md bg-[var(--surface)] border border-[var(--border)] text-[var(--text-primary)] text-sm
chat_message.input_container: flex items-end gap-2 p-4 border-t border-[var(--border)]
chat_message.input: flex-1 px-4 py-2.5 rounded-2xl text-sm bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] border border-[var(--border)] text-[var(--text-primary)] placeholder:text-[var(--text-muted)] focus:outline-none focus:border-[var(--accent)] resize-none max-h-32 overflow-y-auto
chat_message.send_btn: w-9 h-9 rounded-full bg-[var(--accent)] flex items-center justify-center text-white hover:bg-[var(--accent-hover)] transition-colors duration-150
chat_message.typing_indicator: flex items-center gap-1 px-4 py-2 text-sm text-[var(--text-muted)]
chat_message.notes: Sent messages right-aligned with accent bg. Received left-aligned with surface bg. Rounded-2xl is acceptable ONLY for chat bubbles. Timestamps below each message or grouped.

pricing_card.default: rounded-lg border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--surface)] p-6 space-y-6
pricing_card.featured: rounded-lg border-2 border-[var(--accent)] bg-[var(--surface)] p-6 space-y-6 relative
pricing_card.featured_badge: absolute -top-3 left-1/2 -translate-x-1/2 px-3 py-1 rounded-full text-xs font-medium bg-[var(--accent)] text-white
pricing_card.feature_unavailable: flex items-center gap-2 text-sm text-[var(--text-muted)] line-through
pricing_card.notes: Featured plan: border-2 accent + badge. CTA in featured = primary button. CTA in others = secondary button.

calendar_cell.container: min-h-[100px] border-r border-b border-[var(--border)] p-1.5 hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] transition-colors duration-100
calendar_cell.day_today: text-xs font-medium text-white bg-[var(--accent)] w-6 h-6 rounded-full flex items-center justify-center mb-1
calendar_cell.event: px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded text-[10px] font-medium truncate cursor-pointer mb-0.5
calendar_cell.notes: Month calendar view. Today's date gets accent circle. Events are small pills in the cell. +N more link for overflow.

image_gallery.item: relative aspect-square rounded-lg overflow-hidden group cursor-pointer
image_gallery.image: w-full h-full object-cover group-hover:scale-105 transition-transform duration-300
image_gallery.overlay: absolute inset-0 bg-black/0 group-hover:bg-black/30 transition-colors duration-200 flex items-center justify-center
image_gallery.lightbox_overlay: fixed inset-0 bg-black/90 z-50 flex items-center justify-center
image_gallery.lightbox_nav: absolute top-1/2 -translate-y-1/2 w-10 h-10 rounded-full bg-white/10 flex items-center justify-center text-white hover:bg-white/20 cursor-pointer
image_gallery.notes: Grid layout with aspect-square. Hover: subtle zoom + overlay. Click opens lightbox. Arrow keys navigate in lightbox. Escape closes.

comment_thread.author: text-sm font-medium text-[var(--text-primary)]
comment_thread.action_btn: text-xs text-[var(--text-tertiary)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] cursor-pointer transition-colors duration-100
comment_thread.reply_container: ml-11 mt-3 pl-4 border-l-2 border-[var(--border)] space-y-4
comment_thread.notes: Avatar left, content right. Replies indented with left border. Actions: Reply, Edit (own), Delete (own). Textarea for new comment with avatar.

sidebar_detail.description: Right-side detail panel that slides in over content (e.g., entity detail, filters).
sidebar_detail.panel: fixed right-0 top-0 h-screen w-[480px] max-w-full bg-[var(--bg-primary)] border-l border-[var(--border)] z-40 overflow-y-auto
sidebar_detail.header: sticky top-0 flex items-center justify-between px-6 py-4 border-b border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--bg-primary)] z-10
sidebar_detail.close: w-8 h-8 flex items-center justify-center rounded-md text-[var(--text-muted)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)]
sidebar_detail.section_title: text-xs font-medium uppercase tracking-wider text-[var(--text-tertiary)] mb-3
sidebar_detail.notes: Slides in from right. Overlay behind. Close on Escape. Sticky header. Sections with field rows for metadata display.

onboarding_checklist.title: text-sm font-semibold text-[var(--text-primary)]
onboarding_checklist.progress_bar: w-full h-1.5 rounded-full bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] mb-4 overflow-hidden
onboarding_checklist.item: flex items-center gap-3 py-2.5 group cursor-pointer
onboarding_checklist.item_checkbox_done: w-5 h-5 rounded-full bg-[var(--success)] flex items-center justify-center text-white shrink-0
onboarding_checklist.item_checkbox_pending: w-5 h-5 rounded-full border-2 border-[var(--border)] group-hover:border-[var(--accent)] transition-colors duration-100 shrink-0
onboarding_checklist.notes: Use for user onboarding, setup wizards, getting-started flows. Progress bar at top. Completed items get green check + strikethrough.

metric_chart_card.title: text-sm font-semibold text-[var(--text-primary)]
metric_chart_card.period_btn: px-2 py-1 rounded text-[var(--text-tertiary)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] cursor-pointer transition-colors duration-100
metric_chart_card.period_btn_active: px-2 py-1 rounded text-[var(--text-primary)] bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] font-medium
metric_chart_card.notes: Card with title, period selector (7d/30d/90d), chart, and legend. Chart area is h-48 standard. Use Recharts or Chart.js. Legend below chart, not inside.

key_value_list.description: Metadata display in two columns. Used in detail pages, sidebars, settings.
key_value_list.label: text-sm text-[var(--text-tertiary)] shrink-0
key_value_list.notes: Label left, value right. Use for: assignee, due date, priority, status, created date. Values can be badges, links, or plain text.

filter_bar.filter_btn: inline-flex items-center gap-1.5 px-3 py-1.5 rounded-md text-sm border border-[var(--border)] text-[var(--text-secondary)] hover:border-[var(--text-tertiary)] transition-colors duration-100 cursor-pointer
filter_bar.filter_btn_active: inline-flex items-center gap-1.5 px-3 py-1.5 rounded-md text-sm border border-[var(--accent)] bg-[var(--accent-muted)] text-[var(--accent)] cursor-pointer
filter_bar.filter_count: w-4 h-4 rounded-full bg-[var(--accent)] text-white text-[10px] flex items-center justify-center
filter_bar.notes: Horizontal row of filter buttons. Active filters show accent styling + count. Clear all link when any filter is active.

data_list.description: Vertical list of items. Alternative to data_table for simpler content or mobile.
data_list.item: flex items-center gap-4 py-3 px-4 hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] transition-colors duration-100 cursor-pointer
data_list.item_icon: w-10 h-10 rounded-lg bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] flex items-center justify-center shrink-0
data_list.notes: Use when items have icon + title + description. Simpler than table. Good for file lists, notification lists, search results.

inline_edit.display: px-1.5 py-0.5 -mx-1.5 rounded text-sm text-[var(--text-primary)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] cursor-pointer transition-colors duration-100 border border-transparent
inline_edit.editing: px-1.5 py-0.5 -mx-1.5 rounded text-sm text-[var(--text-primary)] bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] border border-[var(--accent)] outline-none
inline_edit.notes: Click text to enter edit mode. Enter to save. Escape to cancel. Subtle bg change shows editable. Used for task titles, entity names, etc.

page_layouts._instructions: Each page type has a structural skeleton. The model picks the right layout per page and fills in content. These cover EVERY app type: SaaS, e-commerce, CRM, booking, messaging, marketplace, analytics, help desk, LMS, inventory, social.

dashboard_overview.description: Main dashboard landing page. Works for: SaaS admin, e-commerce, CRM, analytics, help desk, inventory.
structure: Header: page title + optional date range selector or global search
Stat row: 3-4 stat_cards (grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-4)
Primary content: main entity table OR metric chart (60% of remaining space)
Secondary content: activity feed, quick actions, or secondary chart (40%)

responsive.mobile: stat_grid becomes grid-cols-2, main_content becomes single column, secondary stacks below

dashboard_overview.notes: Hierarchy: stats top, primary dominates, secondary supplemental. Never a flat grid of identical cards.

entity_list.description: List/table page for any entity. Works for: tasks, contacts, orders, tickets, students, products, invoices, bookings.
structure: Header: entity name + 'Create New' primary button
Toolbar: search input left + filter buttons center + view toggle (list/grid) right
Data table OR card grid
Pagination at bottom

responsive.mobile: toolbar stacks vertically, search full-width, filters horizontal scroll, table becomes card list

entity_list.notes: Search always left. Filters beside search. Create button always top-right. Table for data-dense views, cards for visual content. ALWAYS include empty state.

entity_detail.description: Single entity detail page. Works for: task detail, contact profile, order detail, ticket view, product page, booking detail.
structure: Breadcrumb: parent list > entity name
Title header: name + status badge + action buttons (edit, delete, share)
Two-column: main content (2/3) + metadata sidebar (1/3)
Main: description, subtasks/line items, comments, activity timeline
Sidebar: key-value metadata (assignee, dates, priority, labels)

responsive.mobile: single column, sidebar collapses to horizontal sections below main content
responsive.tablet: sidebar moves below on medium screens

entity_detail.notes: Sidebar metadata uses key_value_list recipe. Main content sections separated by cards or subtle dividers. Comments at bottom of main column.

settings.description: Settings or profile page. Works for: account settings, team settings, billing, integrations, notification preferences.
structure: Page title ('Settings')
Sidebar navigation (tabs) for sections OR horizontal tabs
Form content: single column, max-w-2xl, grouped sections
Each section: heading + description + form fields
Save button at section bottom or sticky footer

layout_classes.settings_nav_item: block px-3 py-2 rounded-md text-sm text-[var(--text-secondary)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] transition-colors duration-100 cursor-pointer
layout_classes.settings_nav_active: block px-3 py-2 rounded-md text-sm text-[var(--text-primary)] bg-[var(--accent-muted)] font-medium

responsive.mobile: settings nav becomes horizontal tabs above content, form goes full-width

settings.notes: max-w-2xl for forms. Left nav for 5+ sections. Horizontal tabs for 2-4 sections. Group related fields. Save per-section or global save.

auth_login.description: Login page. Also covers: signup, forgot password, email verification, 2FA.
structure: Full viewport centered layout
Card: logo/app name, form fields, submit button
Optional: social login, divider, alternative action link

layout_classes.page_wrapper: min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center bg-[var(--bg-primary)] px-4

auth_login.notes: max-w-sm. Minimal or no visible card border. Background is bg-primary. Social login buttons below form with 'or continue with' divider.

kanban_board.description: Kanban/board view. Works for: task boards, sales pipelines, recruitment stages, content workflows.
structure: Header: board title + view toggle (list/board) + create button
Horizontal scrolling container with columns
Each column: header (title + count) + card list + add button

responsive.mobile: columns stack vertically, board becomes a single-column list with collapsible sections

kanban_board.notes: Board takes full remaining height. Columns scroll horizontally. Use kanban_column recipe. Mobile: switch to list view automatically.

landing_page.description: Marketing/landing page. Works for: product homepage, feature pages, company pages.
structure: Navbar: logo left, nav links center, CTA button right
Hero: large heading + subheading + CTA button(s)
Social proof: logos row or stat counter
Features: 3 features in a grid (icon + title + description)
Testimonials or metrics section
CTA section: repeat main call to action
Footer: link columns + copyright

layout_classes.navbar: flex items-center justify-between px-6 py-4 max-w-7xl mx-auto
layout_classes.hero_sub: text-lg text-[var(--text-secondary)] mt-4 max-w-2xl mx-auto leading-relaxed
layout_classes.social_proof: flex items-center justify-center gap-8 py-8 opacity-50
layout_classes.features_grid: grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-8 max-w-6xl mx-auto px-4 py-20
layout_classes.feature_icon: w-10 h-10 rounded-lg bg-[var(--accent-muted)] flex items-center justify-center text-[var(--accent)]
layout_classes.footer: border-t border-[var(--border)] py-12 px-4 max-w-6xl mx-auto
layout_classes.footer_link: text-sm text-[var(--text-tertiary)] hover:text-[var(--text-primary)] transition-colors duration-100 block py-1

landing_page.notes: max-w-7xl navbar, max-w-4xl hero, max-w-6xl content. Hero text-5xl minimum. Social proof logos at 50% opacity. Testimonials are real quotes from real people (use realistic sample data).

contact_form.description: Contact or inquiry page. Also covers: support forms, feedback forms, application forms.
structure: Two-column: form left (3/5), contact info right (2/5)
OR single-column centered (max-w-xl)
Form: name, email, subject, message, submit
Info: email, phone, address, hours, social links

layout_classes.page_wrapper: min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center px-4 py-20 bg-[var(--bg-primary)]
layout_classes.container: w-full max-w-4xl grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-5 gap-12

contact_form.notes: Form never full-width. Submit button full-width within form. Include success state after submission (swap form for thank-you message).

pricing_page.description: Pricing tiers. Also covers: plan comparison, upgrade flow.
structure: Centered heading + subheading
Optional billing toggle (monthly/annual)
3 plan cards — middle one highlighted as recommended
Each card: name, price, description, feature list, CTA
FAQ section below

layout_classes.cards_grid: grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-6 max-w-5xl mx-auto
layout_classes.faq_question: text-sm font-medium text-[var(--text-primary)] cursor-pointer flex items-center justify-between

pricing_page.notes: Use pricing_card recipe. Monthly/Annual toggle uses toggle_switch. FAQ items are collapsible accordions.

inbox_messaging.description: Messaging/inbox view. Works for: DMs, support tickets, email client, team chat.
structure: Three-column: conversation list (w-80) + message thread (flex-1) + optional detail panel
List: search + conversation previews with avatar, name, last message, timestamp
Thread: header (contact info) + messages + input area
Detail: contact metadata, shared files, actions

layout_classes.conversation_list: w-80 border-r border-[var(--border)] flex flex-col overflow-hidden
layout_classes.list_item: flex gap-3 px-4 py-3 hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] cursor-pointer border-b border-[var(--border-subtle)] transition-colors duration-100
layout_classes.list_item_active: flex gap-3 px-4 py-3 bg-[var(--accent-muted)] cursor-pointer border-b border-[var(--border-subtle)]
layout_classes.list_item_unread: flex gap-3 px-4 py-3 hover:bg-[var(--surface-hover)] cursor-pointer border-b border-[var(--border-subtle)] font-medium
layout_classes.message_header: h-14 flex items-center justify-between px-6 border-b border-[var(--border)]

responsive.mobile: conversation list is full-width, click opens thread as separate view with back button

inbox_messaging.notes: Three-column layout. Conversation list scrolls independently. Active conversation highlighted. Unread conversations bolded with dot. Use chat_message recipe for bubbles.

calendar_view.description: Calendar page. Works for: scheduling, booking, event management, availability.
structure: Header: month/week/day toggle + navigation arrows + today button + create event
Month grid OR week view OR day view
Event details in sidebar or modal on click

layout_classes.calendar_header: flex items-center justify-between px-6 py-4 border-b border-[var(--border)]
layout_classes.weekday_header: py-2 text-center text-xs font-medium uppercase tracking-wider text-[var(--text-tertiary)] border-b border-[var(--border)]

calendar_view.notes: Use calendar_cell recipe for day cells. Week/day views show time slots on left axis. Events are color-coded by category. Click event opens detail.

analytics_dashboard.description: Data-heavy analytics page. Works for: marketing analytics, financial reports, usage metrics, monitoring.
structure: Header: date range picker + comparison toggle + export button
Top row: 4-6 stat cards with sparklines
Main chart: large area/line chart spanning full width
Secondary row: 2-3 smaller charts (bar, pie, table)
Detailed breakdown table at bottom
analytics_dashboard.notes: Data-dense layout. Stats can go up to 6 across. Main chart is full-width and taller (h-64). Use metric_chart_card for secondary charts. All numbers use tabular-nums. Date range picker top-right.

profile_page.description: User or public profile. Works for: user profiles, company profiles, team member pages, portfolio.
structure: Cover/banner area (optional, h-32 to h-48)
Profile header: avatar (xl) + name + title/role + action buttons
Tab navigation: Overview, Activity, Projects, etc.
Tab content area

layout_classes.profile_header: max-w-4xl mx-auto px-6 -mt-10 flex items-end gap-4 mb-6
layout_classes.profile_avatar: w-20 h-20 rounded-full border-4 border-[var(--bg-primary)] bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] flex items-center justify-center text-2xl font-semibold text-[var(--text-secondary)] shrink-0

profile_page.notes: Avatar overlaps cover by -mt-10. Name and actions on same horizontal line. Tab content scrolls below fixed profile header.

checkout_page.description: Checkout/payment page. Works for: e-commerce checkout, subscription purchase, donation.
structure: Two-column: form left (3/5), order summary right (2/5)
Form sections: contact, shipping, payment
Order summary: line items, subtotal, tax, total, apply coupon
Submit: 'Place order' or 'Subscribe' button

layout_classes.container: max-w-5xl mx-auto grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-5 gap-8
layout_classes.summary_card: rounded-lg border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--surface)] p-5 sticky top-6
layout_classes.total_row: flex items-center justify-between py-2 text-base font-semibold text-[var(--text-primary)]

checkout_page.notes: Order summary is sticky on scroll. Stepper at top (Contact > Shipping > Payment). Total always visible. Security badges near payment fields.

file_manager.description: File/document manager. Works for: drive, media library, asset manager, document hub.
structure: Header: breadcrumb path + view toggle (grid/list) + upload button
Toolbar: search + sort + filter by type
Grid of file cards OR list view
File detail panel (sidebar_detail) on click

layout_classes.breadcrumb_path: flex items-center gap-1.5 text-sm text-[var(--text-tertiary)] mb-4
layout_classes.file_grid: grid grid-cols-2 sm:grid-cols-3 md:grid-cols-4 lg:grid-cols-6 gap-4
layout_classes.file_card: rounded-lg border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--surface)] p-3 hover:border-[var(--accent)] transition-colors duration-150 cursor-pointer group
layout_classes.file_preview: aspect-square rounded-md bg-[var(--bg-tertiary)] flex items-center justify-center mb-2 overflow-hidden

file_manager.notes: Grid default, list for dense view. Drag-and-drop for organizing. Multi-select with shift/cmd-click. Right-click context menu. Preview images inline, show file type icon for others.

onboarding_flow.description: Multi-step onboarding or setup wizard. Works for: user onboarding, team setup, integration setup.
structure: Full-screen or max-w-lg centered
Progress stepper at top
Step content: form fields or selection cards
Navigation: Back + Next/Finish buttons
Optional skip link

layout_classes.page_wrapper: min-h-screen flex flex-col items-center justify-center bg-[var(--bg-primary)] px-4 py-12
layout_classes.selection_card: rounded-lg border border-[var(--border)] bg-[var(--surface)] p-4 cursor-pointer hover:border-[var(--accent)] transition-colors duration-150 text-center
layout_classes.selection_card_selected: rounded-lg border-2 border-[var(--accent)] bg-[var(--accent-muted)] p-4 cursor-pointer text-center

onboarding_flow.notes: Use stepper recipe at top. Each step is self-contained. Selection cards for choices (plan type, role, interests). Progress saves between steps.

layout_classes.page_wrapper: min-h-screen flex items-center justify-center bg-[var(--bg-primary)] px-4
layout_classes.title: text-xl font-semibold text-[var(--text-primary)]

error_pages.notes: Centered, minimal. Large error code number. Brief explanation. Primary action: 'Go home'. Secondary: 'Go back'. No cute illustrations — keep it clean.

responsive_rules_app._instructions: Every layout MUST follow these breakpoint rules. The model applies them to every page.
rules: Sidebar: hidden on mobile, show hamburger menu button in header. Sidebar slides in as overlay drawer.
Stat grids: 2 columns on mobile, 4 on desktop. Never 1 column — too much vertical scroll.
Content grids (2/3 + 1/3): single column on mobile, sidebar content stacks below.
Data tables: horizontal scroll on mobile with sticky first column. OR switch to card list view.
Modals: full-width on mobile (max-w-full, rounded-none on bottom), centered on desktop.
Kanban: stack columns vertically on mobile, horizontal scroll on tablet+.
Forms: single column on mobile, 2-column grid for short fields on desktop.
Navigation: bottom tab bar on mobile for primary nav (max 5 items), sidebar on desktop.
Touch targets: minimum 44px height for all interactive elements on mobile.
Font sizes: don't scale down below text-sm (14px) on any screen size.
Padding: p-4 on mobile, p-6 on desktop for page wrappers.
Landing page hero: text-3xl on mobile, text-5xl on desktop.

interaction_patterns._instructions: Standard interaction behaviors that every app must implement. These are not optional.

patterns.name: optimistic_updates
patterns.description: When user creates/updates/deletes, update the UI immediately before the server confirms. Show a subtle undo toast if the action might be accidental.
patterns.applies_to: toggle switches, checkbox changes, drag-and-drop reorder, inline edits

patterns.name: loading_states
patterns.description: Every data fetch has three states: loading (skeleton), success (data), error (alert with retry button). Never show a blank page.

patterns.name: keyboard_navigation
patterns.description: All interactive elements accessible via Tab. Enter/Space to activate buttons. Escape to close modals/dropdowns. Arrow keys in lists and menus.
patterns.applies_to: buttons, links, inputs, modals, dropdowns, menus, tabs

patterns.name: debounced_search
patterns.description: Search inputs debounce by 300ms before firing a request. Show a subtle spinner in the input while loading. Clear button appears when input has value.

patterns.name: infinite_scroll_or_pagination
patterns.description: Lists with >20 items must paginate or infinite scroll. Show 'Loading more...' indicator at bottom for infinite scroll. Never load all data at once.
patterns.applies_to: entity lists, activity feeds, search results, comment threads

patterns.name: confirmation_before_destructive
patterns.description: Delete, remove, revoke, and disconnect actions always show a confirmation dialog. The confirm button names the action ('Delete project') not just 'Confirm'.
patterns.applies_to: delete buttons, remove members, revoke access, disconnect integrations

patterns.name: form_submission_feedback
patterns.description: Submit button shows loading state during save. On success: toast notification + redirect or inline update. On error: inline error messages per field + top-level alert if server error.

patterns.name: relative_timestamps
patterns.description: All timestamps show relative format by default ('2 hours ago', 'Yesterday'). Full date on hover tooltip. Use a consistent library (date-fns formatDistanceToNow).
patterns.applies_to: activity feeds, comments, messages, audit logs, updated_at fields

patterns.name: copy_to_clipboard
patterns.description: IDs, URLs, API keys, codes — show a copy button. On click: copy to clipboard + show 'Copied!' tooltip for 2 seconds.
patterns.applies_to: reference IDs, share links, API keys, invite codes

data_visualization_rules._instructions: Rules for any chart, graph, or data visualization component.
rules: Use the design system accent color as the primary chart color. Use text-tertiary for grid lines and axes.
Chart backgrounds are transparent (inherit from card bg). Never add a separate bg color to charts.
Always show a loading skeleton while chart data loads. Never show an empty chart area.
Axes labels use text-xs text-[var(--text-tertiary)]. Tick values use tabular-nums.
Tooltips on hover showing exact values. Format numbers with locale-appropriate separators.
Legends go below the chart, not inside. Use small colored squares (w-2 h-2), not circles.
Line charts: 2px stroke, no fill by default. Area charts: 10-15% opacity fill.
Bar charts: rounded-t-sm on bars. 60% width relative to category spacing.
Pie/donut charts: max 6 segments. Group small segments into 'Other'.
Derive display range from data (min/max of actual values), never hardcode date offsets.
Period selectors (7d/30d/90d) go in the card header, right-aligned.
Comparison mode: current period as solid line, previous period as dashed line at 50% opacity.

css_base._instructions: This CSS is injected into globals.css during scaffold. Sets up variables, resets, and base typography.

slop_scan_patterns_design._instructions: Post-build regex patterns to detect AI design slop. Used by _scan_design_quality() in WiringAgent.

patterns.name: raw_tailwind_colors
patterns.message: Uses raw Tailwind color instead of design system CSS variable

patterns.name: heavy_shadows
patterns.message: Uses heavy shadow. Only modal, toast, dropdown, and command palette should use shadow-lg/xl

patterns.name: over_rounded
patterns.message: Uses excessive border radius. Max rounded-xl for modals, rounded-lg for cards

patterns.name: gradient_backgrounds
patterns.message: Uses gradient background on container. Prefer solid colors

patterns.name: missing_transitions
patterns.message: Has hover state without transition. Add transition-colors duration-150

patterns.name: missing_loading_state
patterns.message: Data fetch without corresponding loading state. Every fetch needs a loading/skeleton state

patterns.name: missing_error_state
patterns.message: Data fetch without error handling. Every fetch needs an error state

patterns.name: missing_empty_state
patterns.message: Array rendering without empty state check. Show a message when list has zero items

patterns.name: hardcoded_date_offset
patterns.message: Hardcoded date offset detected. Derive display range from actual data

patterns.name: missing_disabled_state
patterns.message: Button without disabled prop handling. Add disabled:opacity-50 disabled:cursor-not-allowed

patterns.name: non_accessible_click
patterns.message: onClick on non-button element without role='button'. Add role and keyboard handlers

universal_conversion_rules._instructions: These rules apply to EVERY public-facing page. The model must follow all of them regardless of product type, industry, or audience.
above_the_fold: The visitor decides stay-or-bounce in under 5 seconds. The above-the-fold content must answer THREE questions instantly: What is this? Why should I care? What do I do next?
ONE headline. ONE subheadline. ONE primary CTA. That's it above the fold. No feature lists, no testimonials, no secondary content.
The headline must contain a SPECIFIC OUTCOME, not a feature. 'Ship 47% faster' not 'Project management tool'. 'Save 12 hours/week' not 'Automation platform'.
The CTA button text must complete the sentence 'I want to ___'. 'Start free trial' works. 'Submit' does not. 'Learn more' is weak. 'Get started' is generic.
If there's a secondary CTA, it must be VISUALLY subordinate (outline/ghost button) and LOWER commitment (watch demo, see pricing, read docs).
page_structure: Every page follows this emotional arc: PROMISE (hero) → PROOF (social proof / metrics) → MECHANISM (how it works / features) → OBJECTION HANDLING (risk reversal / FAQ) → ACTION (final CTA).
Never put features before proof. The visitor doesn't care how it works until they believe it works.
Every section must answer exactly ONE of: What is it? Why should I care? Does it actually work? Can I trust you? What do I do next?
If a section doesn't serve the conversion goal, remove it. Decorative sections (team photos, office pictures, mission statements) go on /about, not the landing page.
Repeat the CTA at least 3 times on a long page: after hero, after proof, and at the bottom. Same action, same button — just different surrounding context.
copy_rules: Write for scanners, not readers. 80% of visitors never read body text. Headlines, subheadlines, button text, and bullet points are all they see.
One idea per sentence. One argument per section. One action per page.
Use 'you' and 'your', never 'we' or 'our' in headlines. The page is about the VISITOR, not the product. 'Your team will ship faster' not 'We help teams ship faster'.
Numbers beat adjectives. '47% faster' beats 'much faster'. '$29/mo' beats 'affordable'. '4 minutes' beats 'quick setup'. '2,400 teams' beats 'many companies'.
Concrete beats abstract. 'Import from Jira in one click' beats 'Easy migration'. 'See who's blocked before standup' beats 'Improve visibility'.
Short words beat long words. 'Start' not 'commence'. 'Fix' not 'remediate'. 'Ship' not 'deploy'. 'Fast' not 'expeditious'.
Remove every word that doesn't add meaning. 'Start your free trial today and see the difference' → 'Start free trial'.
Headlines: 6-12 words max. Subheadlines: 15-25 words max. Body paragraphs: 2-3 sentences max. Bullet points: 1 sentence each.
Never use 'solution', 'leverage', 'synergy', 'cutting-edge', 'next-generation', 'world-class', 'robust', 'seamless', 'innovative', or 'revolutionary'. These words mean nothing.
visual_hierarchy: Every page has exactly ONE primary visual weight. On a landing page, it's the hero headline. On a pricing page, it's the featured plan. On a signup page, it's the form.
Text size hierarchy: hero headline (text-5xl on desktop, text-3xl on mobile) > section headings (text-2xl) > card titles (text-lg) > body text (text-sm). Never violate this hierarchy.
Color draws attention. Use the accent color ONLY on: primary CTA buttons, active states, key metrics, and status badges. If everything is accented, nothing is.
Whitespace is structural. More space between sections than between elements within a section. Section gaps: 80-100px. Internal gaps: 16-24px.
Max content width: 1280px for full layouts, 960px for content areas, 640px for text-heavy sections, 480px for forms, 380px for auth pages. Text wider than 640px is unreadable.
social_proof_rules: Social proof is not optional. Every public-facing page must include at least ONE form of social proof.
Specific beats generic. Always. '2,417 teams in 43 countries' beats 'Thousands of users worldwide'. A named person with a title beats an anonymous quote.
Testimonials must include: real name, job title, company name, and at least one specific outcome (number, timeframe, or measurable result).
Logo bars use 40-50% opacity. They're passive signals, not attention-grabbers. 5-7 logos max.
Metrics use tabular-nums font variant and large text (text-3xl to text-4xl). The number is the content — the label is secondary.
Place the first social proof element within the first 2 scroll-lengths of the page. Don't make the visitor scroll 5 sections before seeing proof.
trust_and_risk_reversal: Every page with a conversion action (signup, purchase, contact) must include at least ONE risk-reversal element.

Risk reversal hierarchy (strongest to weakest): specific outcome guarantee ('if you don't save 5+ hours') > money-back guarantee ('30-day refund') > free trial ('14 days free') > no-commitment language ('cancel anytime').
Guarantees must be specific. 'Money-back guarantee' is medium strength. '30-day money-back, no questions, no forms, full refund' is strong.
Place risk-reversal near the final CTA — it handles the last objection before the visitor clicks.
Security badges (SOC 2, GDPR, SSL) go near payment forms and signup forms. They answer 'is this safe?' at the moment of commitment.
Trust signals include: company address, phone number, real team photos (on about page), established-since date, total customers, uptime stats.

landing_page_frameworks._instructions: These are the universal conversion frameworks. Each is a psychological structure, NOT a specific layout. The model picks the framework based on the product's relationship to the visitor (are they aware of the problem? are they comparing options? are they ready to buy?) and fills in product-specific content. Every framework includes section-by-section psychology annotations so the model understands WHY each section exists.

authority_dominance.name: Authority and dominance
authority_dominance.when_to_use: When the product has existing traction (customers, metrics, recognizable logos). When the target buyer values security, reliability, and scale. Enterprise SaaS, B2B platforms, infrastructure tools, APIs, developer tools.
authority_dominance.visitor_mindset: Evaluating options. Needs confidence that this is the safe, proven choice.
authority_dominance.psychology: People follow authority. When a page signals that the biggest, most successful organizations already chose this, the visitor's brain shortcuts the decision. Cialdini's Authority Principle + social proof at scale.

section_flow.purpose: Establish dominance immediately
elements: Live status badge: '[X,XXX]+ [customers/teams/companies] and counting' with animated dot — creates social proof + recency
Headline: outcome-focused, includes a number — frames the product as the obvious category leader
Subheadline: expands with scale/credibility — mentions combined customer value, team sizes, or industry authority
Primary CTA: low-risk entry — 'Start free' with risk-removal micro-copy
Secondary CTA: even lower commitment — 'Watch demo' or 'See pricing'
section_flow.psychology_notes: The status badge triggers FOMO before the visitor reads a single word. The headline must make them think 'this is the one.' Two CTAs offer different commitment levels for different buyer readiness.

section_flow.purpose: Anchor large numbers in the visitor's mind
elements: 3-4 metrics in a horizontal row
Categories: total usage/volume, reliability/uptime, customer count, key outcome metric
Each metric: large number (text-3xl tabular-nums) + small label (text-xs uppercase tracking-wide)
section_flow.psychology_notes: Anchoring effect. Multiple large numbers hit simultaneously — the brain processes these as facts, not claims. '4.2M+ tasks completed' sounds like a platform, not a startup.

section_flow.purpose: Handle security/compliance objections before they form
elements: Horizontal row of compliance badges or trust markers
Examples: SOC 2, GDPR, HIPAA, SSO/SAML, uptime SLA, encryption standards
section_flow.psychology_notes: Enterprise buyers have a mental checklist. These badges preemptively check boxes. Position early so the visitor doesn't bounce thinking 'this won't pass our security review.'

section_flow.purpose: Show mechanism (HOW it delivers the outcome)
elements: 3 features in a grid (icon + title + 1-2 sentences)
Each feature framed as OUTCOME not capability: 'Know exactly how fast your team ships' not 'Analytics dashboard'
section_flow.psychology_notes: Rule of three. More than 3 creates decision fatigue. Each feature answers 'what's in it for me?' not 'what does it do?' The visitor doesn't care about features — they care about what features DO for them.

section_flow.purpose: Third-party validation from a high-authority source
elements: Single large quote from senior leader at recognizable company
Include: full name, title, company, team/company size
Quote must include a specific outcome: '34% velocity increase in the first month'
section_flow.psychology_notes: One powerful testimonial from a high-status person converts better than 10 generic ones. The team size makes it credible — this person manages hundreds of people and chose your product.

section_flow.purpose: Close with social proof + action
elements: Headline restating customer count: 'Join [X,XXX]+ teams already [outcome]'
Same primary CTA as hero
Micro-copy with risk removal
section_flow.psychology_notes: The customer count appears for the third time. Repetition creates familiarity. Familiarity creates trust. The CTA at the bottom catches visitors who scrolled the entire page and are now ready.

problem_agitation_solution.name: PAS — Problem, Agitation, Solution
problem_agitation_solution.when_to_use: When the visitor is aware they have a problem but hasn't committed to a solution. When you're replacing a broken workflow, manual process, or hated competitor. Productivity tools, migration plays, process automation, anything fixing a known pain.
problem_agitation_solution.visitor_mindset: Frustrated. Knows something is broken. Looking for confirmation that someone understands their pain.
problem_agitation_solution.psychology: Meet the visitor where they are — in pain. Name the pain specifically. Make it worse by quantifying the cost. Then present relief. This works because it creates an emotional journey: recognition → anxiety → hope → action.

section_flow.purpose: Name the pain the visitor already feels
elements: Headline in error/danger color: direct, confrontational, uses 'you/your'
3 pain points with X/cross icons in red
Each pain point: bold problem name + specific consequence with a NUMBER
Use the visitor's own language — words they'd use complaining to a colleague
section_flow.psychology_notes: Pattern interrupt — most pages start with 'we're great!' Starting with problems is unexpected and creates engagement. The visitor thinks 'yes, that's exactly my problem' which builds instant trust. Naming specific pain with numbers shows you deeply understand their world.

section_flow.purpose: Make the pain more urgent and bridge to the solution
elements: Transition headline as a 'what if' question or future-pacing statement
Optional: quantify the annual cost of the problem (hours wasted × hourly rate = dollar amount)
section_flow.psychology_notes: Questions force the brain to engage — it can't scan past a question. 'What if' creates possibility thinking, bridging from pain to hope. The cost calculation converts abstract frustration into concrete dollars.

section_flow.purpose: Present relief — mirror each problem with a resolution
elements: Headline in success/green color: 'Here's what changes.' or similar
3 solution points with checkmark icons in green
Each mirrors a pain point from above with the resolution + specific outcome metric
Same structure as problems: bold outcome + specific number
section_flow.psychology_notes: Emotional release. After building tension with problems, the solution creates relief. Green signals safety/go. The mirrored structure (3 problems → 3 solutions) creates cognitive closure — every pain has been addressed.

section_flow.purpose: Eliminate the last objection: 'what if it doesn't work?'
elements: Bordered card with guarantee headline + specific terms
Must be MORE specific than 'cancel anytime'
Best: outcome-based guarantee + personal service commitment
section_flow.psychology_notes: The visitor is now emotionally convinced but rationally cautious. Risk reversal handles the rational brain. Specificity makes it believable — 'we'll personally help you migrate back' is a commitment you wouldn't make if you weren't confident.

section_flow.purpose: Action-oriented close with friction removal
elements: Headline: transformation statement (problem state → solution state)
CTA button with outcome verb
Micro-copy: reduce perceived effort (setup time, import ease, no credit card)
section_flow.psychology_notes: Micro-copy after the CTA handles 'but is it hard?' before the visitor can think it. Specific numbers (4 minutes, one click) are more credible than vague reassurance.

social_proof_wall.name: Social proof wall
social_proof_wall.when_to_use: When you have many customers willing to speak. Consumer SaaS, community-driven products, marketplaces, products in crowded categories where the visitor is comparing several options. Also: when your product's value is hard to explain but easy to show through others' experiences.
social_proof_wall.visitor_mindset: Comparing options. Wants to know 'do people like me use this? do they like it?'
social_proof_wall.psychology: Herd mentality — the most primal conversion trigger. When humans see that many other humans made a choice, they assume it's the correct choice. Volume of proof overwhelms skepticism.

section_flow.purpose: Establish momentum with live social proof
elements: Live counter badge: '[XX] [customers] signed up this [period]' with animated indicator
Headline: herd/bandwagon language — 'The fastest-growing teams run on [product]'
Subheadline: name competitors being replaced — shows confidence
Single CTA: curiosity-driven — 'See why teams switch'
section_flow.psychology_notes: Live counter creates urgency without being pushy. Naming competitors targets search intent and shows you're not afraid of comparison.

section_flow.purpose: Passive authority from recognizable brands
elements: 5-7 company logos or names at 40% opacity
Horizontally centered
section_flow.psychology_notes: Low-opacity logos are processed subconsciously. The visitor registers 'big companies use this' without feeling sold to.

section_flow.purpose: Volume of proof from diverse sources
elements: 4-6 testimonial cards in 2-column grid
Vary: job titles (CTO, PM, Designer, Eng Manager), company sizes (startup to enterprise), industries
Every quote must include at least one SPECIFIC outcome (number, timeframe, measurable result)
Include migration stories ('switched from X'), adoption stories ('entire team uses it'), outcome stories ('velocity went up Y%')
section_flow.psychology_notes: Volume overwhelms skepticism. Diverse roles show cross-functional adoption. Specific outcomes make quotes believable — vague quotes ('love this tool!') feel fabricated.

section_flow.purpose: Hard data to satisfy the analytical brain
elements: 3 metrics: transformation metric, time-saved metric, adoption/satisfaction metric
section_flow.psychology_notes: Testimonials are emotional (System 1). Metrics are rational (System 2). Together they satisfy both decision-making systems.

section_flow.purpose: Herd close — make NOT buying feel like the odd choice
elements: Headline: '[X,XXX] [customers] can't be wrong'
CTA: 'Join them' language
section_flow.psychology_notes: 'Can't be wrong' is a rhetorical challenge — the visitor would have to argue that thousands of people made a bad decision.

urgency_scarcity.name: Urgency and scarcity
urgency_scarcity.when_to_use: Product launches, seasonal promotions, products with clear ROI that can be quantified, paid acquisition funnels, retargeting campaigns, beta programs with limited spots.
urgency_scarcity.visitor_mindset: Interested but procrastinating. Needs a reason to act NOW instead of 'later' (which means never).
urgency_scarcity.psychology: Loss aversion is 2x stronger than gain motivation (Kahneman & Tversky). When people believe they might lose an opportunity, they act faster. Time pressure + cost of inaction + easy onboarding = conversion.

section_flow.purpose: Establish time pressure immediately
elements: Full-width accent-colored banner at page top
Content: offer description + SPECIFIC deadline + inline CTA link
Must be a REAL deadline — fake urgency permanently destroys trust
section_flow.psychology_notes: First thing seen. Creates a mental clock. Specific dates (April 10th) are more urgent than 'limited time' because they're concrete and the visitor can't rationalize 'I'll do it later.'

section_flow.purpose: Quantify the cost of inaction
elements: Headline: frame as what they're LOSING, not what they'd gain
Subheadline: do the math — convert hours wasted to dollars burned per year
CTA: time-bound language
Micro-copy: reinforce deadline or offer terms
section_flow.psychology_notes: Loss aversion. 'You're losing $62,400/year' hits harder than 'Save $62,400/year.' The math makes the loss concrete — abstract waste is easy to ignore, specific dollar amounts are not.

section_flow.purpose: Make the action feel effortless
elements: 3-step numbered breakdown of onboarding/setup
Each step: title + one sentence
Include specific time ('4 minutes') and ease markers ('one-click')
section_flow.psychology_notes: The #1 conversion blocker is perceived effort. Three steps with specific details makes adoption feel effortless. Odd numbers (3) feel more natural than even.

section_flow.purpose: Remove the last rational objection
elements: Guarantee with specific terms
Outcome-based if possible ('if you don't see results in X days')
section_flow.psychology_notes: The visitor is now thinking 'but what if it doesn't work?' The guarantee answers this before they can talk themselves out of it.

section_flow.purpose: Final urgency pulse
elements: Headline: restate deadline
CTA: 'Lock in' or 'Claim' language — implies the offer might disappear
Micro-copy: restate guarantee
section_flow.psychology_notes: 'Lock in' implies scarcity. The visitor has seen the deadline 3 times now (banner, hero, CTA). The repetition creates genuine urgency.

competitor_comparison.name: Us vs. them — competitor takedown
competitor_comparison.when_to_use: When there's a dominant incumbent the visitor is likely comparing you to. Products positioned as simpler/cheaper/better alternatives to Jira, Salesforce, HubSpot, Mailchimp, etc. Migration-focused positioning.
competitor_comparison.visitor_mindset: Already using a competitor and unhappy. Googling '[competitor] alternative'. Knows what they want — needs proof you're better.
competitor_comparison.psychology: Anchoring + contrast. By placing your product next to a known competitor, you anchor expectations to the competitor's weaknesses. Green checks vs. red X's create an instant visual verdict that bypasses analytical thinking.

section_flow.purpose: Acknowledge the competitor and validate the visitor's frustration
elements: Headline: name the competitor. 'You already know [Competitor] is [problem]. Here's why teams switch.'
Subheadline: reframe value — same capabilities, less pain
Primary CTA: migration-focused — 'Switch in under 5 minutes'
